comments.body: Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.

I noticed that this PR didn´t compile when trying to vendor in the c/image@main.
@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL

> bin/podman grew by 177352 bytes; max allowed is 51200.

I think we need to live with that growth but I want somebody else to wave. @Luap99 ?

> > bin/podman grew by 177352 bytes; max allowed is 51200.
> 
> I think we need to live with that growth but I want somebody else to wave. @Luap99 ?

Added the label, I don't think there is much we can do about it and it blocks vendoring

Sure, LGTM. Fair number of API-related deprecations in there, wow.

/lgtm
/hold

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon*, *renovate[bot]* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@baude @mheon @Luap99 are these very stubborn flakes?  I restarted 3 times but seem to get a persistent `Error: cannot start VM c736be6f3593: VM already running or starting`
The OS X tests are an expected failure (and allowed failure) at the moment. But we expect the tests to actually run, and fail in testing itself.
